faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Summit home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool clothing, cashmere, or other natural fabrics, silky webbing in closet corners, small cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying near wardrobes or storage areas. You may also notice damaged rugs, upholstered furniture, or felt materials. Our Summit technicians can perform a thorough inspection to confirm the presence of clothes moths and identify the extent of the infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Summit, NJ?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Summit typically range from $550 to $5,000 depending on the size of your home, severity of infestation, number of treatment areas, and specific methods required. A single-room treatment may cost $550–$1,200, while whole-house treatments for severe infestations in larger Summit properties can reach $3,000–$5,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and fabrics in Summit?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use targeted treatments specifically designed to eliminate clothes moths while being safe for your family, pets, and delicate fabrics. Our methods include pheromone traps, fabric-safe insecticides, and heat treatments when appropriate. We take extra care with valuable textiles, antiques, and heirlooms common in Summit homes, and provide detailed preparation and aftercare instructions to ensure the best results without damage.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Summit home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular cleaning and vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in sealed containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and ensuring proper ventilation in storage spaces. We recommend dry cleaning or washing woolens before storage, inspecting vintage or secondhand items before bringing them home, and scheduling annual inspections. Our Summit team can provide a customized prevention plan based on your home's specific risk factors.
